About: It is about Morse code transmissions sent at very high speeds.
ConsistsOf: It consists of copying or sending of five-character groups of text.
It consists of simulations of amateur radio activity.
It consists of copying of amateur radio call signs.
HasA: It has three main competitive events.
It has separate competitions for reception of just the twenty-six letters of the Latin alphabet.
It has separate competitions for reception of just the ten Arabic numerals.
It has separate competitions for reception of a mixed content of letters, numbers, and some punctuation symbols.
It has separate competitions for sending five character groups of just the twenty-six letter of the Latin alphabet.
It has separate competitions for sending five character groups of just the ten Arabic numerals.
It has separate competitions for sending five character groups of a mixed content of letters, numbers, and some punctuation symbols.
It has a software program called RufzXP.
It has a software program called MorseRunner.
It has 9 categories.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by challenges to accurately receive and copy Morse code transmissions.
It is characterized by challenges to accurately send Morse code transmissions.
It is characterized by being most popular in Eastern Europe.
It is characterized by sponsorship from the International Amateur Radio Union.
It is characterized by competitors distinguishing between call signs sent during several simultaneous transmissions.
It is characterized by competitors recording text by hand on paper.
It is characterized by competitors recording text by typing on a computer keyboard.
It is characterized by transmission tests where competitors must try to send five character groups in Morse code as fast as possible.
It is characterized by the simulation of a "pileup" situation in on-air amateur radio operating.
HeldIn: It was held in Moscow, Russia.
It was held in Hannover, Germany.
It was held in Neerpelt, Belgium.
It was held in Siófok, Hungary.
It was held in Europe.
It was held in Herceg-Novi, Montenegro.
It was held in Bar, Montenegro.
It was held in Beatenberg, Switzerland.
It was held in Obzor, Bulgaria.
It was held in Pordenone, Italy.
HeldOn: It was held on 31 May - 2 June, 2013.
It was held on 21 till 25 September 2016.
IsA: It is a form of radiosport.
KnownFor: It is known for challenging amateur radio operators to accurately receive and copy Morse code transmissions sent at very high speeds.
It is known for being most popular in Eastern Europe.
It is known for international competitions sponsored by the International Amateur Radio Union.
MemberOf: It is a member of the International Amateur Radio Union.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Moscow, Russia.
It occurred in Hannover, Germany.
It occurred in Neerpelt, Belgium.
It occurred in Siófok, Hungary.
It occurred in Europe.
It occurred in Herceg-Novi, Montenegro.
It occurred in Bar, Montenegro.
It occurred in Beatenberg, Switzerland.
It occurred in Obzor, Bulgaria.
It occurred in Pordenone, Italy.
PartOf: It is part of amateur radio.
PerformedBy: It was performed by amateur radio operators.
UsedAs: It is used as a form of radiosport.
It is used as a competition.
UsedFor: It is used for challenging amateur radio operators to accurately receive and copy Morse code transmissions sent at very high speeds.
It is used for simulating amateur radio activity.
It is used for distinguishing between call signs sent during several simultaneous transmissions.
It is used for recording text by hand on paper.
It is used for recording text by typing on a computer keyboard.
It is used for transmission tests.
It is used for sending five character groups in Morse code as fast as possible.
It is used for simulating a "pileup" situation.
UsedIn: It is used in amateur radio.
UsedTo: It is used to accurately receive and copy Morse code transmissions.
It is used to accurately send Morse code transmissions.
It is used to distinguish between call signs sent during several simultaneous transmissions.
It is used to record text by hand on paper.
It is used to type text on a computer keyboard.
It is used to send five character groups in Morse code as fast as possible.
It is used to simulate a "pileup" situation.
